title: "FORE!" — the one word you must never swallow
microFact: A golf ball leaves the club at 200+ km/h. 'Fore!' is not embarrassment — it's the sport's safety siren.
lesson:
If your ball is flying anywhere near people, yell "FORE!" — loud, immediately, no hesitation. Add direction if useful: "Fore right!" It warns anyone in the landing zone to cover their head and turn away. A ball to the head causes real injuries; a shout costs nothing.

Beginners often go quiet from embarrassment when they hit a wild one. Experienced golfers read that silence as reckless, not shy. Shouting fore instantly signals you understand the culture.

If you hear "fore": do NOT look up at the sky to find the ball. Turn away, crouch, cover your head with your arms.

Other safety habits: never stand ahead of someone hitting, never swing when someone is close, and wait until the group ahead is clearly out of range — if you can reach them, you wait. "I never thought I'd reach" is the oldest bad excuse in golf.

quiz.question: You hear someone yell 'FORE!'. What do you do?
options: Look up to spot the incoming ball
Turn away, crouch, and cover your head
quiz.explanation: Never look up — turn away and protect your head and neck with your arms.
